tracks.meaning: the border or boundary line between two areas, regions, or countries
the outer limit or brink of a physical surface, such as the edge of a wound or the edge of a bone
a sharp or keen quality in a voice, manner, or competitive advantage
the outermost nodes or devices in a network closest to end users, as in edge computing
a slight advantage or superior position over a competitor
the sharp cutting surface of a blade or tool
a state of nervous tension or anxiety, as in being on edge
a state of irritability or tension, often from nervousness or stress
a competitive advantage or superiority in a market or negotiation
the outer boundary or perimeter of a two-dimensional or three-dimensional object
to move gradually or carefully in a specified direction
the advantage a casino or bettor has over opponents in the odds
